edge: The conflict in human-object interaction is due to the limited availability of supervised data, which hinders the prediction of a vast number of possible interaction combinations in real life.
The conflict in human-object interaction arises from the challenge of predicting a vast number of possible interaction combinations in real life with limited supervised data.
Current methods trained from closed-set data predict human-object interactions as fixed-dimension logits, limiting their scalability to open-set categories.
OpenCat reformulates HOI prediction as sequence generation, enabling the prediction of novel interaction classes utilizing the open-set vocabulary of the language modeling framework.
Inspired by the success of vision-language pre-training, a large amount of weakly-supervised data related to human-object interaction is collected from image-caption pairs.

Several auxiliary proxy tasks, including soft relational matching and human-object relation prediction, are devised to pre-train the model using weakly-supervised data related to human-object interaction.
OpenCat significantly boosts human-object interaction performance, especially on a broad range of rare and unseen categories.

HOI methods have struggled to achieve open-category capability due to deficiencies in prediction manner and supervised data. OpenCat reformulates HOI learning as a sequence generation task to achieve open-category prediction on a large number of tail and unseen HOI classes.
Approach involving projecting the input image into fixed-dimension logits through a classifier restricts the ability to identify new HOI triplets.
Language models are more suited to predict free-form texts, while other works explore to generate visual outputs using a single language modeling objective.
HOI learning is reformulated as a language sequence generation problem to leverage an open-set vocabulary.
